# @zakkster/lite-headless/popover

createPopover(options) -> PopoverHandle

## Options

Inherits from createDialog. Additional:

- placement: 12 values         -- 'top'|'bottom'|'left'|'right' x {default, -start, -end}
- offset: number = 8           -- gap between anchor and content (px)
- flip: boolean = true         -- try opposite side when main-axis overflows
- shift: boolean = true        -- slide along cross-axis to stay in boundary
- boundary: 'clipping'|'viewport'|HTMLElement = 'clipping'

Default modal: false (overrides dialog default).

## Returned

Same as DialogHandle plus:
- attachAnchor(el): off   -- positioning origin; defaults to trigger if not set
- attachArrow(el): off    -- gets data-side, inline left/top (cross-axis offset)

## Slot conventions (for <lite-popover>)

- [data-trigger]: opens popover on click
- [data-anchor]:  (optional) positioning anchor distinct from trigger
- [data-content]: the popover panel
- [data-arrow]:   (optional) arrow element; gets data-side + inline position
- [data-close]:   closes popover on click (multiple allowed)

## <lite-popover> attributes

- open                                    -- reflects state, reactive
- placement                               -- e.g. 'bottom-start'
- offset                                  -- e.g. '8'
- no-flip                                 -- flip:false
- no-shift                                -- shift:false
- modal                                   -- modal:true (default is false)
- no-escape, no-outside, container, transition -- same as dialog

## States exposed on content + arrow

- data-open: present when open (boolean)
- data-status: 'closed' | 'opening' | 'open' | 'closing'
- data-side: 'top' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'right' (effective placement after flip)
- data-align: 'start' | 'center' | 'end'

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
